Meta Data v9

Event Name/Title

Krishnizing The Senses

Event Date

26 Dec 1989

Event Location

Mumbai

Event Type

Temple - BG class

Transcriber

Sunita charamurthimath

Completed

28 Jul 2020

Source Audio

https://www.radhanathswamimedia.com/Lec/1989/89-083-Krishnizing%20The%20Senses%20(Mumbai)-BG_10_36-dt_89_12_26.mp3

Source Video

e.g.

Verse

BG 10.36

Tags – Places

Vrindavan,Govardhan hill

Tags – Pastimes

Lifting the govardhan hill, Krishna protects parikshit maharaj when in womb

Tags – Persons

Krishna, Rama, Narada, Bhakti Siddhanta Saraswathi , srila prabhupada, sri Chaitanya maha prabhu

Tags – Themes

Chanting holy name of Lord , finding Krishna , competitive nature of humans,oppulance ,Krishna all attractive, Adventours,strength,Intelligence, cheaters and honest, Bhagwat Gita, Shrimad Bhagavatum

Lecture Transcription

Om namah bhagvate vasudevaya

dyūtaṁ chalayatām asmi
tejas tejasvinām aham
jayo ’smi vyavasāyo ’smi
sattvaṁ sattvavatām aham

[Bg 10.36]

[I am also the gambling of cheats, and of the splendid I am this slender I am victory I am adventure and I am the strength of the strong.]

Purport by His Divine Grace Srila A.C Bhakti Vedanta Swami Prabhupada

There are many kinds of Cheaters all over the universe. Of all cheating processes gambling stands supreme and therefore represents Krishna. As the supreme Krishna can be more deceitful than any mere man. If Krishna choses to deceive a person, no one can surpass him in His deceit. His Greatness is not simply one sided, it is all sided. Among the victorious he is victory, he is the splendour of the splendid among the enterprising and industrious he is the most enterprising, the most industrious among adventurers he is the most adventurous, and among the strong he is the strongest was when Krishna was present on Earth, no one could surpass him in strength. Even in his childhood, he lifted Govardhan hill. No one can surpass him in cheating. No one can surpass him in splendour, no one can surpass him in victory. No one can surpass him in enterprise and no one can surpass him in strength.

Translation: I am also the gambling of cheats, and of the splendid I am this slender I am victory I am adventure and I am the strength of the strong

[Mangalacharan]

The opulence of the Absolute

The tenthth canto or the tenthth chapter of the Bhagavad Gita is entitled, “The opulence of the absolute”. Of course, the opulence of the Supreme Absolute Truth, Shri Krishna is infinite and undefatigable, and undescribable. Because, as described in Śrī Īśopaniṣad, oṁ pūrṇam adaḥ pūrṇam idaṁ , Krishna, the Absolute Truth is perfectly complete. He is the cause of all causes. Everything is within him, and nothing exists outside of him. And yet his divine personality is the source of everything, parasya śaktir vividhaiva śrūyate , [Śvetāśvatara Upaniṣad 6.8]

That everything exists are the eternal energies, which are eternally emanating from the eternal personality, of the Absolute Truth. So to understand the Personality of Godhead is inconceivable. Therefore, it is said, Achinta shakti, that the lord has inconceivable fortresses, and the mind of the modal man with his relative ability to reason and to grasp concepts, can never in truth, understand the opulence of the Absolute.

Krishna attracts our Heart

So therefore, in this chapter of Bhagavad Gita, Lord Shri Krishna, out of his infinite kindness and mercy is giving us a description in such a way that he is using examples of this world. He is giving us examples of the things that we have all perceived, through our sense perception, things we can easily grasp with our mind and he is explaining how he is the essence and the best in each of these categories, in this way, he was just giving us a hint of his Supreme Absolute nature. Here, he is describing that he is the gambling of cheats. You see, Krishna is coming to attract everyone's mind to him. Somehow or other, the name Krishna means all attractive. That means whatever category of piety or impiety, if you understand the essence of everything you are seeking for pleasure is Krishna. And you will be attracted to Krishna.

How to get purified?

Our Spiritual master explains in this chapter, Krishna says I am the taste of water. But actually he is saying I am the taste of liquid, Srila prabhupada use to say, if a man is addicted to drinking wine and he fakes due to his conditioning, that wine is the sweetest and most wonderful flavour and it gives the most pleasant feeling. Then you tell them, that you meditate that Krishna is the taste of your wine, it is the truth. Krishna is the cause of all causes. Nothing exists outside of his energies. So when they drink the wine, if they are meditating out, ah, this is so nice. And Krishna is the taste that I'm appreciating here. Then by thinking of Krishna in this way, gradually they will become purified and they will lose all their desire to drink wine and they will take nice prasad instead. It is said in Bhagavatam

akāmaḥ sarva-kāmo vā
mokṣa-kāma udāra-dhīḥ
tīvreṇa bhakti-yogena
yajeta puruṣaṁ parama

[SB 2.3.10]

[A person who has broader intelligence, whether he be full of all material desire, without any material desire, or desiring liberation, must by all means worship the supreme whole, the Personality of Godhead.]

‘That whether you are desiring all material enjoyment, whether you have no desire for material enjoyment, whether you desire liberation, or whether you desire pure devotional service, for whatever mentality or conditioning you may be in, approach Krishna, worship Krishna. Remember Krishna’Because Krishna is the all pure

om apavitraḥ pavitro vā
sarvāvasthāṁ gato ’pi vā
yaḥ smaret puṇḍarīkākṣaṁ
sa bahyābhyantara-śuciḥ,

[Garuda Purana]

‘Whether you are completely impure or whether you are completely pure, if you simply remember the beautiful lotus eyed Personality of Godhead, Shri Krishna, if you simply remember his name, you will become purified from within and without.’

Giving up desires

Our Guru Maharaj also would sell someone who was smoking cigarettes; you should give up the smoking of cigarettes. But as long as you cannot stop, you should be thinking that Krishna what it isn't the cigarette that I like is Krishna. And by remembering Krishna, with every puff, within a very short time, you will lose all your desire for smoking cigarettes.

Pure Devotee of Lord are honest

So, somehow or other, in whatever condition of life we are in, the goal is to remember Krishna, because simply by that remembrance, we become purified. So there is a class of men, who are gamblers and cheaters. Ah, as a matter of fact, it is not only a class of man, it is practically the whole society. Bhakti Siddhanta Saraswathi Thakur used to say this is a society of the cheaters in the cheater. But there is a class of men who are honest enough to admit. They're dishonest. Ah, that makes sense. Everyone in this world is dishonest. The only person in this world that is thoroughly honest is a pure devotee of Lord Hari why?

bhoktāraṁ yajṣa-tapasāṁ
sarva-loka-maheśvaram
[Bg 5.29]

‘That means that Krishna is the proprietor of everything. If we claim anything to be our own, we are dishonest before God, before the truth. Everything is meant for God's enjoyment. If we consider ourselves to be the enjoyer of anything or everything in this world, we are dishonest. So, in this way, everyone is dishonest.’ This material world is called Durga, which means in Sanskrit prison. When you go to a prison, it is very unlikely to find honest people. But actually, in my experience, I give lectures at prisons very often, and I find the people in prison are more honest than the people outside of the prison. Why? Because they admit that they're dishonest. They're honest about they're being dishonest. But the people in this world they think they're honest, and they want others to be thinking they're honest, so they're dishonest about their dishonesty. But people who are thoroughly dishonest who particularly take the position of a thief who even against all social principles, they engage in pious activities. They know they're dishonest and they're very proud of it. Amongst cheats, a person who can cheat the other cheats is considered the greatest cheat. And he's very highly adored and recognized by other cheaters.

But Krishna is the greatest cheater. He says of gambling I am the chief. Because you see in this material world, everyone without exception, who is trying to cheat Krishna, is ultimately cheated by Krishna.

How Krishna’s external energy cheats us?

A cheater promises you everything and ends up taking everything away. Right? Isn't that the way of a cheater? Cheater will say oh, I will sell you this very nice tape recorder. Very good deal. You just give me ten thousand rupees and you will have best happy tape recorder. And then you give him ten thousand rupees, and you take it home, and it does not work. Aah! What do you say? He say I've been cheated. He promised me everything and took everything away and I have nothing. Isn't that a good cheater? Isn't that what Krishna does everyone through maya through his material energy, he promises you everything. And in the end, he takes everything in and you have nothing. This whole world is just one promise after another.

Give your valuable human energy, give your money, give your life, this human life of yours just dedicated to to prosperity and fame and prestige in a nice house in a nice family in a nice television and nice sense gratification of all varieties. And what happens we give all our energy everything and then we realize that we have nothing in the form of death, cruel death.

Krishna says I am death personified in this chapter. He takes everything away and you realize that you have been cheated. If you try to cheat Krishna, you will be cheated. Because he is the ultimate cheat, the greatest cheat.

But, he goes on and explains that of the splendid I am the splendour, whatever we see in this world, whether we are in the modes of goodness, passion or ignorance, according to the modes of material nature, we are under the conception or under the conditioning of we are thinking a particular thing to be beautiful. A person in a mode of ignorance has a very, very low taste of what is beautiful. A person in the mode of goodness, has a completely another conception of what is beautiful. But Krishna says, whatever your conditioning is, whatever you said that is see that is splendid, he is the splendour of it.

Human civilization of moths

Try to remember Krishna in this way. If you are attracted to a beautiful man or a beautiful woman, why be attracted to the form to the degree, you're attracted to the form, you are like a moth attracted to fire, because that very form will burn you will burn your consciousness to ashes in due course of time. You have seen the moth, when he is attracted to the flame of the fire. He likes very much. Sometimes, if the flame is on the other side of the window, the moth will be going [ummh ummh ummh], just smashing trying to break through the window, practically crushing his body because he's so attracted, he'll do anything to get to that flame. A moth only lives, sometimes one or two nights they have very short lifespan. And sometimes, they dedicate their whole life just smashing against a window trying to get to the flame. And somehow or other when they finally come to the flame, what happens, [spisshhh,] they are burnt to ashes, there's nothing left. They realize as they're being burnt to death, that this is not what I expected it to be. But human life is just a human civilization of moths. Because the forms of this material creation that attract us, ultimately, we are consumed by that attraction and our human life is spoiled, it is burnt.

Krishna is the splendour

But, if we simply understand, according to how Krishna is explaining in this verse, that Krishna is the splendour of the splendid. He is the beauty of the beautiful. Then when we see something beautiful, we think, “Oh, it is the essence of Krishna, his existence in here that is making this so attractive. How wonderful! Are you Krishna? “, How wonderful that by a tiny spark of your energy, you're appearing in such a way that's completely capturing my mind. Then we are Krishna conscious. As long as we are seeing things in this way, there is no danger in this material world.

Maharaj Parikshit been protected by Krishna and finding Him

Maharaja Parikshit, when he was living within the womb of his mother, and Aswathama through the brahmastra to kill him. At that time, the child in the womb saw the beautiful form of Krishna come to protect him. And after seeing the beauty of Krishna, he was named parikshit, which means one who is constantly observing in all circumstances. He was trying to find Krishna everywhere. In the Bible that is said by Lord Jesus Christ ‘seek and you shall find.’ If you're always looking for Krishna, you will always find Krishna. If you're always looking for the fire, you will always find the fire.

So in all things, that are splendid in this world, it is not that we have to become like a certain class of Yogi's that we completely block our senses from the objects. Like today, one gentleman was explaining to me, one famous Yogi that he had heard about Ah, and he was explaining how this man lives in a cage correct and no one is allowed to touch him. And he never talks, what else does he do? He will never talk, he will not hear anything, he will not look at things, you will not communicate with anyone out of fear of being entangled by attachment. In.. in from the material point of view, is very correct, because when you allow your senses to communicate with the objects of the senses, you become very much attached. But obviously, this life is not very pleasurable, nor is it very practical.

Bhakti yoga the highest platform

So, therefore, the Bhakti Yogi, he is, according to Bhagavad Gita, on the highest platform of yoga, and it is the most practical platform of yoga. Don't deny your senses from communication with the objects of the senses. But you have to Krishnizingyour senses, by learning how to perceive Krishna, by learning how to remember Krishna everywhere, in everything, Krishna says in Gita for, for one who sees me everywhere, and everything in me, for me, I for him, I am never lost, nor is he ever lost to me.

If you're looking for Krishna everywhere, you will find him everywhere. Wherever you go, whatever you do, Krishna is there. And he reveals Himself, according to how you approach him. Krishna says, I am victory. Everyone wants to be victorious in whatever they do. How many people want to be a failure?

Competitive nature of humans for material pleasure

Whether, we're students, or whether we're businessmen, or whether we're soldiers or politicians, or housewives, or whether we're children playing games, in whatever field of activity we are in, we want to be victorious. Generally, this is the aah, incentive that keeps people driving forward in this world have so many problems. Just like children, when they are playing games, their nature is they're very competitive. Correct. When you swim, you want to swim faster than all your friends, right? When you run you want to run, faster than all your friends.

Of course, in regard to studies, sometimes we don't really care whether we get better grades. Ha Ha [Maharaja Laughs]

Some students children, but when it comes to games, they all want to win. Why? because there is this natural tendency to enjoy victory. So here to help such people Krishna says, ‘I am victory.’ That that victory we are always seeking is Krishna. Imagine how Krishna conscious you can be, if you just think like this. When such you didn't know when Sachidanand is having swimming race with his friends, he's after victory. He's working so hard. Every object of his body is just struggling and straining to win the race. Aah Correct. And while he's doing it, he's thinking victory. I want to win I want to win. Now what have you remember that, victory is Krishna? Then you'll be thinking, I'm racing to Krishna. It's Krishna, I want. Every aspect of your body is completely immersed in Krishna. In trying to attain Krishna, ah, these cricket matches, aah Pakistan is playing India. All these so many thousands of people are going to watch, I don't know, if it’s, if its athletics or politics, but it's whatever it is. They're they're trying very hard to win to be victorious and they're subject themselves to be injured, and to they're just pounding each other with sticks and balls and they're just working so hard, with only one thing in mind ‘victory.’

Understanding, Krishna is Victory, Adventure, Strength, Splendour, and Intelligence!

Now, can you imagine, if they just understood this one verse of Bhagavad Gita, Krishna says,” I am victory”. And they're conscious with all the energy that what they're trying to do is get Krishna, who is victory? Ah, if they could, if the cricket players of India, even if they're playing Pakistan, it doesn't matter, if they're, if they understand this one verse where Krishna says, I am victory. To the extent, that they are hungry, and anxious and eager for victory to that extent, after one game, they would all be self-realized souls. It is a fact. If they would simply understand that Krishna is victory after that one game of completely just immersing your mind and total energy and seeking victory. They're conscious to Krishna is that victory by the end of the game? They'll just go have peaceful smiles and they'll say, ”Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare /Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare”]. Now welcome to join Radha Gopinath temple.

Aah its fact, Krishna is giving everyone he says, I am adventure. How many people like adventure RPG some people like adventure? Why do people watch television and go to cinemas simply because they want to be entertained entertained by adventure? Krishna says I am adventure. That excitement that you're seeking through adventure. If you're simply conscious that that excitement is Krishna. To the degree you want the excitement to that degree you become self-realized. Krishna says, I am the strength of the strong. Everyone is very impressed by people who are strong. But, Krishna is describing that factually. This is not please understand, this is not an artificial imposition on the mind. This is the fact this is the truth. Krishna is victory he is the splendour of the splendid, He is adventure. He is the strength of the strong. If we do not see it that way, we are an illusion. He is the intelligence of the intelligent. When somebody does something and reveals great feats of strength. Sometimes he goes down in history for that.

Aah, People are videos attracted to strength whatever little or great strength we have that strength is coming from the same place that everything is emanating.

Krishna is the source of everything

Krishna says,

ahaṁ sarvasya prabhavo
mattaḥ sarvaṁ pravartate
iti matvā bhajante māṁ
budhā bhāva-samanvitāḥ

[Bg 10.8]

[I am the source of all spiritual and material worlds. Everything emanates from Me. The wise who perfectly know this engage in My devotional service and worship Me with all their hearts.]

He says that ‘I am the source of all spiritual and material worlds. Everything is emanating from me. The wise who knows this is perfectly engaged in my devotional service and worship me with all their hearts.’ Please try to understand everything is emanating for Krishna from Krishna. Whatever strength you have, it is Krishna strength. He's just giving you a little particle of his infinite strength. And he's giving you the choice of how you want to use it. Whatever knowledge you have, Krishna is the source of all knowledge. He's giving you a tiny little particle of his knowledge. And he's giving you the choice of how you want to use it. Whatever beauty you have, understand, it is just infinitesima, tiny little spark of the Supreme opulence of God's beauty. And he's giving you that spark and giving you the choice of who to use it for and how to use it. Whatever wealth you have, whether you're a popper on the street, or whether you are a very, very wealthy. In person, you should understand that the wealth is coming from the same place, everything is emanating. It is just a little speck of the wealth of God that he's temporarily allowing you to borrow. And he's giving you the choice of how to use it.

Krishna explains of all wonderful things in this creation. It is all just emanating from a tiny spark of my splendour. And Krishna, in this chapter of Bhagavad Gita, is so mercifully giving us a chance to become completely self-realized in a most practical way, living in this everyday world. By simply learning, how to see that Krishna is the source of everything!

How to remember Krishna?

He is the goal of everything that we are striving for, even in our everyday life. And by simply remembering Krishna, our consciousness becomes utterly purified and we can attain the supreme goal of life. Krishna consciousness. And the easiest and most sublime way of always remembering Krishna, along with seeing according to the vision that Krishna is so kindly giving us here in Bhagavad Gita, is to chant his Holy name.

When we attentively chant his Holy name Krishna, Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare/Hare RAMA Hare Rama Hare Hare[Maharaj chants the maha mantra], or any of the munificent names of the Absolute Truth through this process, because God is not different than his name, we are remembering Krishna. And to the degree, we can a concentration within our chanting to that degree, we are remembering Krishna when we are chanting his name. And in all circumstances, in all walks of life, we can remember Krishna by simply chanting,his holy name.

Therefore, Lord Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu has bestowed his blessing upon all the people of this Kali Yuga by giving this most sublime process

ceto-darpaṇa-mārjanaṁ bhava-mahādāvāgni-nirvāpanaṁ
 [Cc. Antya 20.12]

The name of God is our constant companion, our constant friend. Wherever we are, whether we are in the most dangerous circumstances or in the most luxurious conditions, we can transcend the trap of Maya that we are in by simply calling out the name of God. And the name of God appears wherever his devotee wants, simply we desire to call out the name of God and Krishna appears instantly, in that place at that time. So, let us take advantage of this most precious opportunity that is given by God. And let us always remember Krishna, and never forget Krishna. By following this simple principle, whoever we are, whatever our condition of consciousness, by simply understanding this verse of Bhagavad Gita, we can attain the supreme perfection of life in this life. Thank you very much.

Hare Krishna!

For any questions,

Q&A Transcription

[Devotee:] You said like, aaah, in wine, you should like aah the taste of wine you should see Krishna?

[Maharaja:] No, No! You should not drink wine. That's for people who can't give it up. That's a gradual way of bringing them to Krishna consciousness. For you, you should not do like that. Whaaa

[Devotee:] addicted to many other ways of sense gratifications, thennn if see this way. They will never give it up. I mean

[Maharaj:] If you see it that way, you won't ever do it. You'll experience the higher taste of Krishna, Another words, through this verse Krishna is giving everyone the opportunity of being purified.

My dear Vaishnava, you're a doctor, correct? That means you have what a PhD or master's degree?

[Devotee:] graduation

[Maharaja:] So uhh graduation! Hmmaah [Maharaj clearing his throat] so, if I start giving you instructions that are meant for one a first standard. Do you think you should spend all your time studying basic arithmetic? Isn't that a waste of your time? But for someone in the first standard, don't you think it's very important that he fully engrossed his mind in basic arithmetic. So for one who is a drunkard, for one who is engrossed and cannot give up sense gratification? He is understand, he has an almost elementary preliminary condition of spiritual development. He's like in the first standard, so Krishna is teaching them just whatever you do you see Krishna, Krishna is the taste of your wine. Ah. But if you're initiated by a bona fide spiritual master, and you understand philosophically the goal of life, and you've taken sacred vows, and to go back to the first standard is drinking wine to taste Krishna, ah , it is just a waste of your time. Ah, you understand, yes!

[Devotee:] what I understand from with what he said is, giving those people who are drinking wine. Those people who are addicted to wine and smoking cigarettes. They do encourage them by telling that okay the taste you are getting is , enjoyment what they are getting is Krishna, then they will continue thinking about it. And, instead of reducing it ah line and ah smoking ah drinking getting addicted, alcohol abusiveInstead of helping out you are making situation worst!

[Maharaj:] have you ever tried it.

[Devotee:] No, I don’t drink.

[Maharaj:] Ah, It won’t get become worse for telling them to give it up. But until you can give it up, if you cannot give it up, then you understand that Krishna factually is the essence of everything is you know, is he not the taste of wine, the taste of everything. Isn’t it? Is Anything separate from Krishna? It's not a it's not a lie. It's a fact to use the taste of wine. If they cannot give it up, because they're too attached to that taste, we're telling them, you should give it up. It's not good for your spiritual life. But until you can give it up. The process of giving it up is while you're drinking, you meditate how actually this is Krishna. Now, if Krishna was an ordinary material word, and Krishna was an ordinary material person, then what would happen is they would get worse and worse. But because Krishna is all pure, is he just a spiritual process? That's what you're forgetting. Because, Krishna is all pure by remembering Krishna. And by directly linking your consciousness to being attracted to Krishna, in whatever form, you become purified, and you lose the desire for these activities in the mode of ignorance. Drinking alcohol is in the mode of ignorance. By becoming purified you you rise through the modes of passion to the mode of goodness to the transcendental platform. Ah

[Devotee:] but the boy continues to drinking, initially they can think of Krishna, but alcohol is such that after some time they lose their conscious so Krishna, remembering Krishna, for some time will not arise so

[Maharaj:] But he's gonna do it anyway. We're not encouraging anyone to drink. We're ordering everyone to give up drinking. But what if he is drinking? What are you going to do? Just tell him Oh, go to hell, I can't help you. Oh, we're giving him a chance. He's gonna get intoxicated anyways, become unconscious anyway. But at least until he becomes unconscious, he's thinking of Krishna. And through this process, he's gradually going to give it up. He's gonna have a higher purpose of life. You understand? Krishna is expertly giving everyone a chance to go back to Godhead.

[Maharaj:] ah

[Devotee:] I want to ask you some questions which I can’t answer in front of other religion people rather to say, as I told you , I [39.42, not clear ] represent say country which is totally Islamic,

[Maharaj:] hmm

[Devotee:] so they generally ask, that Rama or Krishna, which you surrender, you believe to be God, weather they are prophet or they are God?

[Maharaj:] God

[Devotee:] Shiva [some person in the background saying, not clear 39.59,]

[Devotee:] whether they are prophet or God?

[Maharaj:] They are God. According to according to this, we have to understand who everyone is according to the Scripture, not according to our own sentiments.

[Devotee:] They say there is two God?

[Maharaj:] Ah

[Devotee:] They says there is two God?

[Maharaj:] No. There is one God, but that one God is not like you and me. You are not God, I am not God. God is so great, he is so glorious. That he can expand himself in many, many forms, although he is one. To, to engage in various loving relationships with his various devotees of different mentalities, people who want to approach God with love with one particular sentiment, God may appear in that way just to satisfy all of his desires. Another person who wants to approach God with love in another way, God will appear in that way.

[Devotee:] But if God is so powerful, what is the need to come to earth?

[Maharaj:] He has no need to come on the earth.

[Devotee:] Rama?

[Maharaj:] Rama has absolutely no need to come on earth. Krishna has absolutely no need to come on earth, as we need him to come on this earth. And therefore out of mercy, he is coming to fulfill our needs.

[Maharaj:] ah, just like if a wealthy man sees people starving in the street, does he ever need to feed them? Does he? He doesn't need to go into the ghettos. When a when a multimillionaire goes into the ghettos, to feed the poor people just even need to come into the ghettos to give them food, he has no need. But the people in the ghetto only have a need. And he's so mercifully coming to fulfil their needs. We need God .God does not need us. And God out of his mercy, out of his love, out of his kindness, he descends to this world, to attract our minds back to him by displaying wonderful pastimes, by giving wonderful instructions, which are meant to attract our minds to that place where we can find real happiness and real love.

Just like you are a common man! I am a common man! We been play so many different roles right within our own life. When you're at home, you're a father. When you're at home, you're a husband? Do you deal with your wife the same way you deal with your children? It's different. What happens when you go to your business? Do you deal with your rear business colleagues the same way you deal with your children? And do you deal with your customers the same way you deal with your business colleagues? Ah. So you see you have so many different positions and you have so many ah different relationships in which you appear very much different. Ah, so God is the same way according to the need on the earth, when there's a demon who needs to be killed like Ravana. And Krishna comes in the form of Ram with his bow and arrow and he kills them. Ah. When he has beautiful cowherd girls who want to play with him and dance with him, he appears in his original form playing the flute, to dance with them. You think the gopis want to dance with someone with a bow and arrow in the forest of Vrindhavan and they want to dance with someone or the flute? Ah. So you see, Rama and Krishna the same person there's no difference, except they're simply taking different that one Supreme God is simply taking different roles according to different times and places to fulfill the to re-establish the principles of religion, to attract the hearts of the pious and to annihilate the wicked. Now in the Quran, it is specifically explained that Muhammad is prophet.

[Devotee:] Yes

[Maharaj:] So therefore, we accept that he is prophet but in the Bhagavad Gita, and in the Ramayana, it is specifically explained that yes, there are many prophets. Narada muni is a prophet it describes many prophets, but it also describes that Krishna and Ram are the original source of everything, the Supreme Absolute Truth. They are God.

[Devotee:] But their belief is different.

[Maharaj:] Not different! Their belief is different. But the science is the same science. It is a question of whether we understand the real meaning of our science or Religious Science. Thank you very much.
